# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment YAML for our Python web application. The app runs a Flask API on port 5000. We need 3 replicas for high availability. Keep the configuration simple for quick deployment to our production cluster.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: flask-api-deployment
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: flask-api
    environment: production
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: flask-api
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: flask-api
        environment: production
    spec:
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        runAsGroup: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
        seccompProfile:
          type: RuntimeDefault
      containers:
        - name: flask-api
          image: flask-api:1.0.0
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5000
              protocol: TCP
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            runAsNonRoot: true
            runAsUser: 1000
            runAsGroup: 1000
            capabilities:
              drop:
                - ALL
          env:
            - name: FLASK_ENV
              value: "production"
            - name: FLASK_DEBUG
              value: "0"
            - name: SECRET_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: flask-api-secrets
                  key: secret-key
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: flask-api-secrets
                  key: database-url
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "100m"
              memory: "128Mi"
            limits:
              cpu: "500m"
              memory: "256Mi"
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health
              port: 5000
              scheme: HTTP
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 3
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health
              port: 5000
              scheme: HTTP
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 20
            timeoutSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 3
          volumeMounts:
            - name: tmp-volume
              mountPath: /tmp
      volumes:
        - name: tmp-volume
          emptyDir:
            sizeLimit: "64Mi"
      serviceAccountName: flask-api-sa
      topologySpreadConstraints:
        - maxSkew: 1
          topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
          whenUnsatisfiable: DoNotSchedule
          labelSelector:
            matchLabels:
              app: flask-api
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: flask-api-sa
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: flask-api
automountServiceAccountToken: false
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: flask-api-service
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: flask-api
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: flask-api
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 5000
      protocol: TCP
      name: http
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: flask-api-network-policy
  namespace: production
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: flask-api
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
    - Egress
  ingress:
    - from:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              name: production
      ports:
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 5000
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              name: production
      ports:
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 5432
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector: {}
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
